name: opensearch-mcp
instructions: Use this backend for indexed evidence search, aggregation, timeline analysis, ingest, and enrichment. It is not an independent reference source; use reference-providing backends for grounding.

tools.name: opensearch_search
tools.description: Search indexed evidence with query_string syntax (supports time_from/time_to, offset pagination).
tools.when_to_use: Use for targeted searches across already indexed case evidence.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid before evidence has been ingested or when you need independent reference grounding.
tools.output_notes: Returns indexed case records; use tools/list for query syntax and schema details.

tools.name: opensearch_count
tools.when_to_use: Use to size a search result set before retrieving records.
tools.output_notes: Returns counts only; follow with search or aggregate for evidence details.

tools.name: opensearch_aggregate
tools.description: Group indexed evidence by a field (terms aggregation) with an optional query filter.
tools.when_to_use: Use for scoping distributions such as event codes, users, hosts, processes, or IPs.
tools.output_notes: Aggregation output identifies pivots but is not itself a finding.

tools.name: opensearch_get_event
tools.description: Retrieve a single indexed document by its _id.
tools.when_to_use: Use when a search or timeline result gives a specific document ID to inspect.
tools.output_notes: Returns one indexed event/document for evidence review.

tools.name: opensearch_timeline
tools.description: Event count over time as a date histogram (supports time_from/time_to).
tools.when_to_use: Use to find activity spikes and temporal windows for deeper investigation.
tools.output_notes: Timeline buckets are scoping aids; inspect underlying events before conclusions.

tools.name: opensearch_field_values
tools.description: List unique values for a field (terms aggregation) with an optional query filter.
tools.when_to_use: Use to discover field values before writing narrower searches.
tools.output_notes: Returns value inventory for indexed evidence fields.

tools.name: opensearch_status
tools.description: OpenSearch cluster connectivity and per-case index status.
tools.when_to_use: Use as the backend health/readiness check and to confirm per-case index state.
tools.output_notes: Health/status output only; it is not case evidence.

tools.name: opensearch_shard_status
tools.description: Shard usage and capacity headroom across the cluster.
tools.when_to_use: Use for operational readiness before large ingest or when indexing fails.
tools.output_notes: Administrative capacity signal; not investigation evidence.

tools.name: opensearch_case_summary
tools.description: Complete coverage overview of indexed evidence for a case — the first call in any indexed investigation.
tools.when_to_use: Use after ingest or at session start to understand indexed coverage and gaps.
tools.output_notes: Coverage gaps guide next actions; verify important claims with underlying records.

tools.name: opensearch_inspect_container
tools.description: Inspect a forensic container (E01/zip/etc.) before ingest to preview its contents.
tools.when_to_use: Use during evidence survey before deciding what to ingest.
tools.output_notes: Preview output helps plan ingest and does not modify evidence.

tools.name: opensearch_ingest
tools.description: Discover and ingest forensic artifacts into OpenSearch. dry_run=True by default — the agent must explicitly set dry_run=False to write.
tools.when_to_use: Use after examiner approval to index evidence for structured search.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid setting dry_run=False until the target evidence and ingest plan are clear.
tools.output_notes: Mutating operation scoped to the OpenSearch index; dry runs should precede writes.

tools.name: opensearch_ingest_status
tools.description: Check running/recent ingest operations for a case ("*" for all cases).
tools.when_to_use: Use to monitor ingest progress or troubleshoot recent ingest runs.
tools.output_notes: Operational status only; inspect indexed records for evidence.

tools.name: opensearch_enrich_intel
tools.description: Enrich indexed evidence with OpenCTI threat intel. Async — returns immediately with a run_id.
tools.when_to_use: Use after indexed indicators exist and CTI context would help prioritize pivots.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid when OpenCTI is unavailable or when indicators are speculative.
tools.output_notes: Starts asynchronous enrichment; follow status/results before relying on output.

tools.name: opensearch_enrich_triage
tools.description: Run Windows baseline (windows-triage) enrichment on indexed data.
tools.when_to_use: Use after indexed Windows artifacts exist and baseline context can prioritize anomalies.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid treating UNKNOWN baseline enrichment as malicious by itself.
tools.output_notes: Starts enrichment over indexed data; verify interesting results against source records.

tools.name: opensearch_list_detections
tools.description: Query the Security Analytics plugin for Sigma rule detections; suggests Hayabusa alternatives.
tools.when_to_use: Use to identify indexed Sigma/Security Analytics detections for triage pivots.
tools.output_notes: Detection hits require validation against source events and surrounding context.

tools.name: opensearch_host_fix
tools.description: Correct a wrong host.id mapping across all indexed documents (reindexes affected docs). Scoped to OpenSearch index/alias state.
tools.when_to_use: Use only when indexed host identity is known to be wrong and needs correction.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid before confirming the canonical host mapping with the examiner or evidence.
tools.output_notes: Mutates indexed OpenSearch documents only; it does not alter original evidence.
